title: Handle static function variables
body:
It adds static function variables handling.
It is done by moving a static variable to the upper scope, in which static variables are handled correctly.
Unfortunately, variables without lifetime explicitly specified are sometimes classified incorrectly (for example in static functions). I tried to fix it, but some other problems occurred, for example:
* when a static variable was moved outside a task that is not inlined, the warning: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/f6b101d98b22e484097ba7fe53177804ab2d4d5d/src/V3Task.cpp#L152 was raised. But according to 23.9 of the Standard, it is a legal reference. I would say that it is a problem with references, not with static variables.
* there was a problem when a call of a static function was used for initializing a parameter: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/f6b101d98b22e484097ba7fe53177804ab2d4d5d/test_regress/t/t_param_array3.v#L20. There was an error that expression is not constant.

There where also other problems. I decided to leave it as it is now.
I fixed the classification of for loop variables (they are always automatic), but I think it doesn't change anything currently.
